artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a university-wide GenAI policy and practice page, student AI guidance, academic integrity guidance, programme guidance, and library research guidance. A journal editorial PDF and a news/event page were rejected because they were not suitable university policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: Oxford Brookes student guidance states that students are required to declare which AI tools they used and how they used them in Moodle, then paste the emailed receipt into an appendix at the end of the assignment.

artifacts.claimText: Oxford Brookes student guidance says AI tools should not replace the student as author and warns that gaining an unfair advantage using AI tools may breach academic conduct regulations.

artifacts.claimText: Oxford Brookes describes its university-wide position on generative AI in teaching, learning, and assessment as a progressive embrace-and-adapt approach, with module leaders having discretion to advise on AI use in module assessments.

artifacts.claimText: Oxford Brookes guidance says students and staff have access to Microsoft Copilot through an academic institutional licence, but does not recommend uploading confidential or protected data to Copilot because it does not meet Brookes' information-security good-practice expectations.

artifacts.claimText: Oxford Brookes guidance for schools, programmes, and modules says GenAI principles can be applied across disciplines and should be discussed at programme, course, and module level, with modules addressing equity and academic rigour and programmes advised to develop GenAI literacy and authentic assessment.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Oxford Brookes students are required to use the form in Moodle to declare which AI tools you have used and how you have used them. You will be emailed a receipt copy of your completed declaration which you must then paste into an appendix at the end of your assignment.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: AI can be used at Oxford Brookes under the following conditions

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Remember that AI tools should not be used to replace you as the author of your work. If you gain an unfair advantage using AI tools, you may breach the Oxford Brookes academic conduct regulations.

artifacts.sourceTitle: GenAI University wide policy and practice - Oxford Brookes University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Oxford Brookes has taken what JISC would describe as a progressive 'embrace and adapt' approach to generative AI. Currently: Module leaders have the discretion to advise on AI use in the assessment for their modules; Module handbooks should include up to date information on using GenAI in research or assessment; Students are asked to declare their use of AI on submission of assessment and check with module leaders before using it, in line with QAA guidance.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Oxford Brookes position on the use of Generative AI in teaching, learning and assessment

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Oxford Brookes students and staff have access to the data-secure Microsoft Co-pilot AI chatbot, available through a Microsoft academic institutional licence. However, we do not recommend you upload any confidential or protected data or information because Co-pilot does not comply with Brookes' expectations of good practice with regard to information security.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Make safe choices when choosing AI software; Oxford Brookes recommends Microsoft Co-pilot

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for Schools Programmes and Modules - Oxford Brookes University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: These principles can be applied in every subject discipline and programme area. Their specific application should be discussed and agreed at programme/course and module level. Every module should: Principle 1: Ensure equity in student access to GenAI; Principle 2: Uphold academic rigour and integrity. Every programme is advised to: Principle 3: Develop students' GenAI literacy and skills; Principle 4: Adopt authentic assessment.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow student-facing academic-integrity claim directly supported by the official AI guidance page.
Approved with wording that preserves the source's may-breach language and does not overstate into a blanket prohibition.
Approved as a university-wide source-status and tool-treatment claim grounded in the official staff policy/practice page.
Approved as a privacy/security claim; it does not treat Copilot as unrestricted approved-tool policy.
Approved with programme/module scope retained; not represented as a binding rule for every individual assessment.

artifacts.title: Oxford Brookes University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl found official Oxford Brookes guidance covering student AI declaration, academic integrity risk, a university-wide embrace-and-adapt GenAI position, cautious handling of protected data in AI tools, and programme/module-level teaching guidance. Claims remain agent-reviewed candidates only.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The AI declaration form and Moodle course were login or form resources and were not used as evidence beyond official public pages linking to them.
Repository editorial PDFs and news/event pages were rejected as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
